Title: UTF-8 Implementation Notes For ODBC
Description: ODBC wide-character functions (e.g., SQLDescribeColW) always return correctly encoded wide string identifiers.
For narrow ODBC functions (e.g., SQLDescribeCol), identifiers stored in UTF-8 are:
- Converted from UTF-8 → Unicode → narrowed using the current database character set.
Virtuoso extends the ODBC standard by forcing result-set metadata functions (SQLTables, SQLTablesW, etc.)
to return SQL_NVARCHAR instead of SQL_VARCHAR for identifier columns.
Most applications map SQL_NVARCHAR to SQL_C_CHAR, letting the driver convert to the proper narrow encoding.
However, narrow-only applications (like MS Query) may receive untranslatable characters ('?')
if identifiers contain characters not representable in the current narrow character set.
Examples: SQLDescribeColW(...)  -- Returns wide string identifier correctly
ODBC metadata functions return SQL_NVARCHAR columns
